rationale: Builds on the current best by keeping the training path unchanged and only refining the final int8 quantizer where recent near-misses were strongest: projection weights. For projection matrices, quantization now chooses per row between the existing mean-centered offset, a midpoint-centered offset, and no offset, instead of forcing one tensor-wide center. That targets heterogeneous row asymmetry in the most quantization-sensitive tensors without repeating the broader all-matrix multi-center change that regressed. I also removed the now-unnecessary quant-aware LR helper to keep the script under the 1500-line cap.
title: Selective Projection Row-Center Choice
